free_text: Measurements of gamma-ray emission induced by protons
on fluorine and lithium
Department of physics.
Universidad Autonoma de Madrid.
Parque Cientico de Madrid.
Elastic
scattering measurements.
The target consisted in a 49 mug/cm**2 LIF-film
evaporated on a self-supporting thin C-target, further
coated with a 19 mug/cm2 Au layer.
In order to obtain the excitation functions and
cross-sections, experimental results were normalized by
the Rutherford elastic backscattering of protons from
Au.
Ge detector connected to the vacuum of the target
chamber, placed at an angle of 135 degrees to the beam
axis, 207.5-mm distant from the target and subtending
an angle of about 17 degrees.
To detect elastic scattered protons.
The HVEE 5 mV Tandetron accelerator
See sample.
During the measurements proton beam currents
were in the range 20-40-nA, depending on beam energy,
in order to keep the count rate low enough to reduce
pile-up effects.  Each measurement was allowed to
continue until obtaining at least 3000 counts in the
elastic scattering peak of protons on Au.
Dead time corrections were always less than 15% for the
gamma-detector, whereas they were negligible for the
particle detector (<0.5%).
Data tables were receive from Dr. M.Chiari
The contributions to the error in the
cross-section value from Rutherford cross-section on
Au.
The uncertainty of area density ratio.
The uncertainty of particle detector solid
angle.
The uncertainty of gamma detector absolute
efficiency).
Adding linearly this contributions to the statistical
errors (less than 2%), a 15% conservative estimate for
the uncertainty of the various differential
cross-sections is obtained.
S B
Data tables were receive from Dr. M.Chiari.
Last checking has been done.
SD: INSTITUTE code corrected.
